islands.name: Real Estate Row
islands.description: Learn property investing: auctions, rent, flipping, REITs, and how location drives value.

learning_objectives: Understand property auctions and bidding strategy
Learn rental yield and passive income concepts
Understand how location affects property value
provenance.originality_notes: Theme: tropical real-estate row with distinct districts (not a board). Verb: auction bidding + renovation choices (not roll-and-buy). Structure: quest-driven learning with NPC mentors (not competitive property accumulation). Blends auction mechanics with resource management through guided NPC scenarios.

areas.name: Auction Yard
areas.description: Properties go to the highest bidder. Learn to spot deals and avoid overpaying.

areas.name: Rental District
areas.description: Row houses and apartments. Manage tenants, collect rent, and handle repairs.

areas.name: REIT Tower
areas.description: A gleaming skyscraper. Invest in real estate without owning property directly.

items.name: Property Deed
items.description: Proof that you successfully purchased a property at auction.

items.name: Rental Ledger
items.description: Records of rental income, expenses, and net cash flow.

items.name: REIT Share Certificate
items.description: A share in a diversified real estate investment trust.

items.name: Real Estate Medallion
items.description: Mastery of property investing. Unlocks the next island!

quests.title: Auction & Flip
quests.description: Buy a property at auction, renovate it, and sell for a profit. Learn the buy/rent/flip loop.
quests.hint: Talk to Auctioneer Al to learn about bidding, then visit Flipper Fiona for renovation tips.

quests.title: Passive Income Pipeline
quests.description: Understand rental yield, REIT dividends, and how location affects property value.
quests.hint: Manage rentals with Landlord Larry, then compare REITs with Rachel at the tower.

nodes.speaker: Auctioneer Al
nodes.text: Welcome to the Auction Yard! Every property here starts at a base price — your job is to bid smart. Overpay and you'll never make a profit. Want to learn the ropes?

nodes.speaker: Auctioneer Al
nodes.text: The key is knowing the After-Repair Value (ARV). That's what the property will be worth after renovations. Never bid more than 70% of ARV minus repair costs. Now go talk to Flipper Fiona — she's the renovation expert.

nodes.speaker: Auctioneer Al
nodes.text: No rush! The auction runs all day.

nodes.speaker: Flipper Fiona
nodes.text: I've flipped 30 houses! The secret? Budget your renovation tightly and know when to walk away from a money pit. Ready to try the property auction simulator?

nodes.speaker: Flipper Fiona
nodes.text: Number one: emotional bidding. Number two: underestimating repair costs. Number three: ignoring location — a great house in a bad neighborhood won't sell.

choices.text: Got it — let's try the auction!

nodes.speaker: Landlord Larry
nodes.text: Rental income sounds like free money, but there's a LOT to manage — tenants, maintenance, vacancies. The key metric is Net Operating Income: rent minus all expenses.

choices.text: How do I calculate if a rental is worth it?

nodes.speaker: Landlord Larry
nodes.text: Use the Cap Rate: NOI divided by purchase price. A 6-8% cap rate is solid. Below 4% and you're barely beating inflation. Head to REIT Tower to see Rachel — she'll show you how to invest in real estate without being a landlord.

nodes.speaker: Landlord Larry
nodes.text: Tenants wait for no one! But sure, come back anytime.

nodes.speaker: REIT Rachel
nodes.text: A REIT — Real Estate Investment Trust — lets you invest in properties like malls, apartments, and offices without owning them directly. They trade like stocks and pay dividends. Want to compare some?

nodes.speaker: REIT Rachel
nodes.text: Here's your first REIT share! REITs must pay out 90% of income as dividends, making them great for passive income. But they're still affected by interest rates and property values.

nodes.speaker: REIT Rachel
nodes.text: REITs are sensitive to interest rates — when rates rise, REIT prices often drop. And you don't get to pick which specific properties the trust buys. But for diversification and passive income, they're hard to beat.

minigames.name: Property Auction Simulator
minigames.description: Bid on properties, estimate renovation costs, and flip for profit. Learn the buy/rent/flip loop.

minigames.name: Equity Snowball
minigames.description: Reinvest your rental income and appreciation each year, or cash it out. Feel how compounding builds a property empire.
